Função para criar um usuário Função para criar um usuário

Precisaremos de uma função para criar um usuário, pois vamos usar a API do ViaCEP para validar o CEP dele. Precisaremos de uma função para criar um usuário, pois vamos usar a API do ViaCEP para validar o CEP dele。 Vamos adicionar essa validação posteriormente. Vamos adicionar essa validaçãoteriormente。

Em lib/my_app/users/create.ex : Em lib/my_app/users/create.ex ：

 defmodule MyApp . Users . Create do alias MyApp . { Repo , User } @type user_params :: %{ first_name: String . t (), last_name: integer , cep: String . t (), email: String . t () } @doc """ Inserts a user into the database. ## Examples iex> alias MyApp.{User, Users} ...> ...> user_params = %{ ...> first_name: "Mike", ...> last_name: "Wazowski", ...> cep: "95270000", ...> email: "mike_wazowski@monstros_sa.com" ...> } ...> ...> {:ok, %User{}} = Users.Create.call user_params ...> iex> {:error, %Ecto.Changeset{}} = Users.Create.call %{} """ @spec call ( user_params ()) :: { :error , Ecto . Changeset . t ()} | { :ok , Ecto . Schema . t ()} def call ( params ) do % User {} |> User . changeset ( params ) |> Repo . insert () end end

Consumindo dados de uma API externa Consumindo dados de uma API externa

Para conseguir consumir dados de uma API externa precisamos de um HTTP client. Para conseguir consumir dados de uma API externa precisamos de um HTTP 客户端。 Podemos usar o Tesla ou o HTTPoison. Podemos usar o Tesla o o HTTPoison。 A vantagem do Tesla é que ele possui middlewares prontos para usarmos. Tesla é que ele possui middlewares prontos para usarmos 的优势。 Neste post vamos usar os dois clients para compará-los. Neste post vamos usar os dois clients para compará-los。

Instalando o Tesla Instalando o 特斯拉

安装或 HTTPoison

行为参数 a função get_cep_info()

Vamos definir um contrato para a função get_cep_info() . Vamos definir um contrato para a função get_cep_info() 。 Quando essa função for implementada ela obrigatoriamente deve receber uma String como parâmetro e retornar sempre uma tupla de {:ok, map()} ou {:error, map()} . Quando essa função for implementada ela obrigatoriamente deve receber uma String como parâmetro e retornar semper uma tupla de {:ok, map()} ou {:error, map()} 。

Em lib/my_app/via_cep/behaviour.ex : Em lib/my_app/via_cep/behaviour.ex ：

 defmodule MyApp . ViaCep . Behaviour do @callback get_cep_info ( String . t ()) :: { :ok , map ()} | { :error , map ()} end

Se tentarmos retornar algo diferente para esta função, receberemos um aviso: Se tentarmos retornar algo diferente para esta função, receberemos um aviso:

HTTP Client com Tesla HTTP 客户端 com Tesla

Vamos colocar o nome do arquivo de tesla_client.ex , pois faremos outro chamado httpoison_client.ex e, desta forma, conseguiremos comparar os HTTP clients de um jeito melhor. Vamos colocar o nome do arquivo de tesla_client.ex , pois faremos outro chamado httpoison_client.ex e, desta forma, conseguiremos comparar os HTTP clients de um jeito melhor。

Em lib/my_app/via_cep/tesla_client.ex : Em lib/my_app/via_cep/tesla_client.ex ：

 defmodule MyApp . ViaCep . TeslaClient do # Ao invés de Tesla.get(), vc vai usar apenas get() use Tesla alias Tesla . Env @behaviour MyApp . ViaCep . Behaviour @base_url "https://viacep.com.br/ws/" # codifica (encode) os parametros para json # e descodifica (decode) a resposta para json automaticamente. plug Tesla . Middleware . JSON def get_cep_info ( url \\ @base_url , cep ) do " #{ url }#{ cep } /json/" |> get () |> handle_get () end # casos abaixo de sucesso e erro defp handle_get ({ :ok , % Env { status: 200 , body: %{ "erro" => "true" }}}) do { :error , %{ status: :not_found , result: "CEP not found!" }} end defp handle_get ({ :ok , % Env { status: 200 , body: body }}) do { :ok , body } end defp handle_get ({ :ok , % Env { status: 400 , body: _body }}) do { :error , %{ status: :bad_request , result: "Invalid CEP!" }} end defp handle_get ({ :error , reason }) do { :error , %{ status: :bad_request , result: reason }} end end

Vamos fazer o teste: Vamos fazer o 睾丸：

 iex ( 1 ) > MyApp . ViaCep . TeslaClient . get_cep_info "95270000" { :ok , %{ "bairro" => "" , "cep" => "95270-000" , "complemento" => "" , "ddd" => "54" , "gia" => "" , "ibge" => "4308201" , "localidade" => "Flores da Cunha" , "logradouro" => "" , "siafi" => "8661" , "uf" => "RS" }} iex ( 2 ) > MyApp . ViaCep . TeslaClient . get_cep_info "95270001" { :error , %{ result: "CEP not found!" , status: :not_found }} iex ( 3 ) > MyApp . ViaCep . TeslaClient . get_cep_info "" { :error , %{ result: "Invalid CEP!" , status: :bad_request }}

HTTP Client com HTTPoison HTTP 客户端 com HTTPoison

没有 HTTPoison não temos um middleware pronto para transformar o json em map ，então vamos precisar usar o Jason.decode() que já vem instalado no Phoenix。

Em lib/my_app/via_cep/httpoison_client.ex : Em lib/my_app/via_cep/httpoison_client.ex ：

 defmodule MyApp . ViaCep . HttpoisonClient do alias HTTPoison . { Error , Response } @behaviour MyApp . ViaCep . Behaviour @base_url "https://viacep.com.br/ws/" def get_cep_info ( url \\ @base_url , cep ) do " #{ url }#{ cep } /json/" |> HTTPoison . get () |> json_to_map () |> handle_get () end defp json_to_map ({ :ok , % Response { body: body } = response }) do { _ok_or_error , body } = Jason . decode ( body ) { :ok , Map . put ( response , :body , body )} end defp json_to_map ({ :error , % Error {}} = error ), do : error defp handle_get ({ :ok , % Response { status_code: 200 , body: %{ "erro" => "true" }}}) do { :error , %{ status: :not_found , result: "CEP not found!" }} end defp handle_get ({ :ok , % Response { status_code: 200 , body: body }}) do { :ok , body } end defp handle_get ({ :ok , % Response { status_code: 400 , body: _body }}) do { :error , %{ status: :bad_request , result: "Invalid CEP!" }} end defp handle_get ({ :error , reason }) do { :error , %{ status: :bad_request , result: reason }} end end

Vamos fazer o teste: Vamos fazer o 睾丸：

 iex ( 1 ) > MyApp . ViaCep . HttpoisonClient . get_cep_info "95270000" { :ok , %{ "bairro" => "" , "cep" => "95270-000" , "complemento" => "" , "ddd" => "54" , "gia" => "" , "ibge" => "4308201" , "localidade" => "Flores da Cunha" , "logradouro" => "" , "siafi" => "8661" , "uf" => "RS" }} iex ( 2 ) > MyApp . ViaCep . HttpoisonClient . get_cep_info "95270001" { :error , %{ result: "CEP not found!" , status: :not_found }} iex ( 3 ) > MyApp . ViaCep . HttpoisonClient . get_cep_info "" { :error , %{ result: "Invalid CEP!" , status: :bad_request }}

Validando o CEP na criação do usuário Validando o CEP na criação do usuário

Em lib/my_app/users/create.ex , vamos alterar a função para passar a validar o CEP do usuário e, também, vamos pegar as informações da cidade e UF. Em lib/my_app/users/create.ex , vamos alterar a função para passar a validar o CEP do usuário e, também, vamos pegar as informações da cidade e UF。 Esta função apresenta um problema mas, vamos melhorar ela depois. Esta função apresenta um problema mas，vamos melhorar ela depois。

 alias MyApp . ViaCep . HttpoisonClient , as: Client ... @spec call ( user_params ()) :: { :error , Ecto . Changeset . t () | map ()} | { :ok , Ecto . Schema . t ()} def call ( params ) do cep = Map . get ( params , :cep ) with { :ok , %{ "localidade" => city , "uf" => uf }} <- Client . get_cep_info ( cep ), params <- Map . merge ( params , %{ city: city , uf: uf }), changeset <- User . changeset (% User {}, params ), { :ok , % User {}} = user <- Repo . insert ( changeset ) do user end end

原文:

Maiqui Tomé 🇧🇷

Posted on May 16

Neste post vamos aprender a consumir dados de uma API externa. No momento da criação do usuário, vamos realizar uma chamada na API do ViaCep para validar o CEP dele, buscando e gravando as informações de cidade e UF desse usuário. Vamos aprender também como evitar fazer chamadas desnecessárias.

Criando o projeto 

$ mix phx.new learning_external_api --app my_app

Link do projeto pronto: https://github.com/maiquitome/learning_external_api

Tabela users

Criando a migration e o schema:

$ mix phx.gen.schema User users first_name last_name email cep city uf

Alterando o schema do user:

Criando o banco de dados e rodando as migrations:

Função para criar um usuário

Precisaremos de uma função para criar um usuário, pois vamos usar a API do ViaCEP para validar o CEP dele. Vamos adicionar essa validação posteriormente.

Em lib/my_app/users/create.ex:

defmodule MyApp.Users.Create do alias MyApp.{Repo, User} @type user_params :: %{ first_name: String.t(), last_name: integer, cep: String.t(), email: String.t() } @doc """ Inserts a user into the database. ## Examples iex> alias MyApp.{User, Users} ...> ...> user_params = %{ ...> first_name: "Mike", ...> last_name: "Wazowski", ...> cep: "95270000", ...> email: "mike_wazowski@monstros_sa.com" ...> } ...> ...> {:ok, %User{}} = Users.Create.call user_params ...> iex> {:error, %Ecto.Changeset{}} = Users.Create.call %{} """ @spec call(user_params()) :: {:error, Ecto.Changeset.t()} | {:ok, Ecto.Schema.t()} def call(params) do %User{} |> User.changeset(params) |> Repo.insert() end
end

Consumindo dados de uma API externa

Para conseguir consumir dados de uma API externa precisamos de um HTTP client. Podemos usar o Tesla ou o HTTPoison. A vantagem do Tesla é que ele possui middlewares prontos para usarmos. Neste post vamos usar os dois clients para compará-los.

Instalando o Tesla

Para instalar o Tesla, adicione ao mix.exs:

defp deps do [ {:tesla, "~> 1.4"}, # opcional, mas recomendado {:hackney, "~> 1.17"} # esse não precisa pois já vem com o phoenix {:jason, ">= 1.0.0"} ]
end

Você pode encontrar a versão mais recente do Tesla em: https://hex.pm/packages/tesla

Instalando as dependências:

Testando o Tesla:

iex> Tesla.get "https://viacep.com.br/ws/01001000/json/"
{:ok, %Tesla.Env{}} iex> Tesla.get "" {:error, {:no_scheme}} iex> Tesla.get "https://exemplo.com" {:error, :econnrefused}

Instalando o HTTPoison

Para instalar o HTTPoison, adicione ao mix.exs:

defp deps do [ {:httpoison, "~> 1.8"} ]
end

Você pode encontrar a versão mais recente do HTTPoison em: https://hex.pm/packages/httpoison

Instalando as dependências:

Testando o HTTPoison:

iex> HTTPoison.get "https://viacep.com.br/ws/01001000/json/"
{:ok, %HTTPoison.Response{}} iex> HTTPoison.get "" ** (CaseClauseError) no case clause matching: [] iex> HTTPoison.get "https://exemplo.com" {:error, %HTTPoison.Error{id: nil, reason: :closed}}

Behaviour para a função get_cep_info()

Vamos definir um contrato para a função get_cep_info(). Quando essa função for implementada ela obrigatoriamente deve receber uma String como parâmetro e retornar sempre uma tupla de {:ok, map()} ou {:error, map()}.

Em lib/my_app/via_cep/behaviour.ex:

defmodule MyApp.ViaCep.Behaviour do @callback get_cep_info(String.t()) :: {:ok, map()} | {:error, map()}
end

Se tentarmos retornar algo diferente para esta função, receberemos um aviso:

HTTP Client com Tesla

Vamos colocar o nome do arquivo de tesla_client.ex, pois faremos outro chamado httpoison_client.ex e, desta forma, conseguiremos comparar os HTTP clients de um jeito melhor.

Em lib/my_app/via_cep/tesla_client.ex:

defmodule MyApp.ViaCep.TeslaClient do # Ao invés de Tesla.get(), vc vai usar apenas get() use Tesla alias Tesla.Env @behaviour MyApp.ViaCep.Behaviour @base_url "https://viacep.com.br/ws/" # codifica (encode) os parametros para json # e descodifica (decode) a resposta para json automaticamente. plug Tesla.Middleware.JSON def get_cep_info(url \\ @base_url, cep) do "#{url}#{cep}/json/" |> get() |> handle_get() end # casos abaixo de sucesso e erro defp handle_get({:ok, %Env{status: 200, body: %{"erro" => "true"}}}) do {:error, %{status: :not_found, result: "CEP not found!"}} end defp handle_get({:ok, %Env{status: 200, body: body}}) do {:ok, body} end defp handle_get({:ok, %Env{status: 400, body: _body}}) do {:error, %{status: :bad_request, result: "Invalid CEP!"}} end defp handle_get({:error, reason}) do {:error, %{status: :bad_request, result: reason}} end
end

Vamos fazer o teste:

iex(1)> MyApp.ViaCep.TeslaClient.get_cep_info "95270000"
{:ok, %{ "bairro" => "", "cep" => "95270-000", "complemento" => "", "ddd" => "54", "gia" => "", "ibge" => "4308201", "localidade" => "Flores da Cunha", "logradouro" => "", "siafi" => "8661", "uf" => "RS" }} iex(2)> MyApp.ViaCep.TeslaClient.get_cep_info "95270001"
{:error, %{result: "CEP not found!", status: :not_found}} iex(3)> MyApp.ViaCep.TeslaClient.get_cep_info "" {:error, %{result: "Invalid CEP!", status: :bad_request}}

HTTP Client com HTTPoison

No HTTPoison não temos um middleware pronto para transformar o json em map, então vamos precisar usar o Jason.decode() que já vem instalado no Phoenix.

Em lib/my_app/via_cep/httpoison_client.ex:

defmodule MyApp.ViaCep.HttpoisonClient do alias HTTPoison.{Error, Response} @behaviour MyApp.ViaCep.Behaviour @base_url "https://viacep.com.br/ws/" def get_cep_info(url \\ @base_url, cep) do "#{url}#{cep}/json/" |> HTTPoison.get() |> json_to_map() |> handle_get() end defp json_to_map({:ok, %Response{body: body} = response}) do {_ok_or_error, body} = Jason.decode(body) {:ok, Map.put(response, :body, body)} end defp json_to_map({:error, %Error{}} = error), do: error defp handle_get({:ok, %Response{status_code: 200, body: %{"erro" => "true"}}}) do {:error, %{status: :not_found, result: "CEP not found!"}} end defp handle_get({:ok, %Response{status_code: 200, body: body}}) do {:ok, body} end defp handle_get({:ok, %Response{status_code: 400, body: _body}}) do {:error, %{status: :bad_request, result: "Invalid CEP!"}} end defp handle_get({:error, reason}) do {:error, %{status: :bad_request, result: reason}} end
end

Vamos fazer o teste:

iex(1)> MyApp.ViaCep.HttpoisonClient.get_cep_info "95270000"
{:ok, %{ "bairro" => "", "cep" => "95270-000", "complemento" => "", "ddd" => "54", "gia" => "", "ibge" => "4308201", "localidade" => "Flores da Cunha", "logradouro" => "", "siafi" => "8661", "uf" => "RS" }} iex(2)> MyApp.ViaCep.HttpoisonClient.get_cep_info "95270001"
{:error, %{result: "CEP not found!", status: :not_found}} iex(3)> MyApp.ViaCep.HttpoisonClient.get_cep_info "" {:error, %{result: "Invalid CEP!", status: :bad_request}}

Validando o CEP na criação do usuário

Em lib/my_app/users/create.ex, vamos alterar a função para passar a validar o CEP do usuário e, também, vamos pegar as informações da cidade e UF. Esta função apresenta um problema mas, vamos melhorar ela depois.

alias MyApp.ViaCep.HttpoisonClient, as: Client
... @spec call(user_params()) :: {:error, Ecto.Changeset.t() | map()} | {:ok, Ecto.Schema.t()} def call(params) do cep = Map.get(params, :cep) with {:ok, %{"localidade" => city, "uf" => uf}} <- Client.get_cep_info(cep), params <- Map.merge(params, %{city: city, uf: uf}), changeset <- User.changeset(%User{}, params), {:ok, %User{}} = user <- Repo.insert(changeset) do user end end

Criando um usuário:

iex(1)> user_params = %{ ...(1)> first_name: "Mike",
...(1)> last_name: "Wazowski",
...(1)> cep: "95270000",
...(1)> email: "mike_wazowski@monstros_sa.com"
...(1)> }
%{ cep: "95270000", email: "mike_wazowski@monstros_sa.com", first_name: "Mike", last_name: "Wazowski"
} iex(2)> MyApp.Users.Create.call user_params CEP: "95270000"
{:ok, %MyApp.User{ __meta__: #Ecto.Schema.Metadata<:loaded, "users">, cep: "95270000", city: "Flores da Cunha", email: "mike_wazowski@monstros_sa.com", first_name: "Mike", id: 1, inserted_at: ~N[2022-05-15 21:38:14], last_name: "Wazowski", uf: "RS", updated_at: ~N[2022-05-15 21:38:14] }}

CEP Inválido 

iex(1)> user_params = %{ ...(1)> first_name: "Mike",
...(1)> last_name: "Wazowski",
...(1)> cep: "123",
...(1)> email: "mike_wazowski@monstros_sa.com"
...(1)> }
%{ cep: "123", email: "mike_wazowski@monstros_sa.com", first_name: "Mike", last_name: "Wazowski"
}
iex(2)> MyApp.Users.Create.call user_params CEP: "123"
{:error, %{result: "Invalid CEP!", status: :bad_request}}

Perceba que foi mostrada a mensagem Invalid CEP! ao invés de mostrar a validação do changeset, alertando que o CEP devia possuir 8 caracteres, desta forma, teriamos a validação sem precisar fazer uma chamada desnecessária para a API externa.

Evitando chamadas desnecessarias para API Externa (apply_action)

Vamos agora validar todos os dados do changeset antes de fazer a chamada para a API do ViaCep.

Testando a validação do changeset sem usar o Repo.insert():

iex(1)> import Ecto.Changeset iex(2)> user_params = %{
...(2)> first_name: "Mike",
...(2)> last_name: "Wazowski",
...(2)> cep: "123",
...(2)> email: "mike_wazowski@monstros_sa.com"
...(2)> }
%{ cep: "123", email: "mike_wazowski@monstros_sa.com", first_name: "Mike", last_name: "Wazowski"
} iex(3)> MyApp.User.changeset(%MyApp.User{}, user_params) |> apply_action(:create)
{:error, #Ecto.Changeset< action: :create, changes: %{ cep: "123", email: "mike_wazowski@monstros_sa.com", first_name: "Mike", last_name: "Wazowski" }, errors: [ cep: {"should be %{count} character(s)", [count: 8, validation: :length, kind: :is, type: :string]} ], data: #MyApp.User<>, valid?: false >}

Vamos alterar o arquivo lib/my_app/user.ex, adicionando a função validate_before_insert:

def validate_before_insert(changeset), do: apply_action(changeset, :insert)

Vamos agora alterar o arquivo lib/my_app/users/create.ex:

@spec call(user_params()) :: {:error, Ecto.Changeset.t() | map()} | {:ok, Ecto.Schema.t()} def call(params) do cep = Map.get(params, :cep) changeset = User.changeset(%User{}, params) with {:ok, %User{}} <- User.validate_before_insert(changeset), {:ok, %{"localidade" => city, "uf" => uf}} <- Client.get_cep_info(cep), %{} = _params <- Map.merge(params, %{city: city, uf: uf}), {:ok, %User{}} = user <- Repo.insert(changeset) do user end end

Agora antes de fazer a chamada para o ViaCep estamos checando todas as validações do do changeset antes:

User-Agent header

Algumas API's externas podem solicitar algo a mais para realizar as chamadas, muitas podem pedir um token, no qual você consegue na documentação, ou no caso da API do github, um User-Agent header:

Com o Tesla facilmente você pode usar o plug Tesla.Middleware.Headers:

No Httpoison:

iex> HTTPoison.get "https://api.github.com/users/maiquitome/repos", [{"User-Agent", "foobar"}]

Leia na documentação do HTTPoison a parte de options.

Então, fique atento a documentação da API na qual você está realizando a chamada.

Conclusão

Em algum momento você vai precisar consumir dados de uma API externa; o que nos dias de hoje é bem normal. Ler a documentação da API é o primeiro passo para o sucesso. Em Elixir temos ótimas ferramentas de HTTP Client, a documentação dessas ferramentas também merece ser lida. Precisamos cuidar para não fazer chamadas desnecessárias em API's externas para não comprometer a performance da nossa aplicação. O próximo passo agora é saber como realizar testes automatizados nessas chamadas externas; assunto para um próximo post.

Items.Title: 如何为您的网站带来免费流量 | How to Drive Free Traffic to your Website
Items.Description:
杰西卡-阿戈耶

发表于拥有网站的人面临的一大挑战是为他们的网站增加流量。 您网站上的访问者数量根据网站的目标而有所不同，这可能是网站的目标是增加销售额、产生高质量的潜在客户、创造机会、提升品牌知名度、产生更多收入或达到志同道合的人，建立一个社区，理由是无限的。

因此，为您的网站带来流量至关重要，您可以拥有最有吸引力的网站，但是，如果您没有网站流量，那它只是一个空壳，一开始就违背了创建它的目的。 有几种方法可以为您的网站带来流量，其中包括： 使用社交媒体、推荐网站、付费搜索、自然搜索、电子邮件通讯、直接流量等。

任何网站的流量都可以提高知名度和参与度，目标是吸引高质量的访问者访问您的网站，这些访问者可能会转化为客户。 在本文中，我们将重点介绍免费为您的网站带来流量的不同有效方法。 让我们看看如何利用免费渠道被发现：

搜索引擎优化
这些搜索引擎旨在根据互联网用户提交的搜索查询收集信息。 呈现的结果称为 SERP（搜索引擎结果页面）。

在搜索引擎上，查询是在一行结果中，通常根据排名。 虽然搜索引擎的算法是独一无二的，并且使用各种复杂的算法来生成搜索结果，但这些算法的一个共同主题是它们采用网页的关键元素，如页面标题、描述标签、URL、内容和关键字密度的网页，从而将该页面放在它在 SERP 上排名的位置。 随着每天创建数百个网站和新网页，为您的网站带来流量的前两种方式之一是通过搜索引擎优化 [SEO]。

以下三种方法可以提高您在任何搜索引擎上的 SEO 排名：

  1. 关键字：页面搜索引擎优化的关键是关键字策略。 关键字是具有重要意义的词。 例如，如果您的网站是关于健康生活的，那么像健身、健康和养生之类的词是应该在您的网站上战略性地出现的关键词。 这些是您网站页面上的展示位置。 当有查询时，搜索引擎会与您网站上的内容进行交互，它们会显示为 SERP。 这就是为什么内容创建是我们的下一个重点。

  2. 搜索引擎优化内容：搜索引擎优化和内容创建齐头并进。 没有内容，SEO 就无法工作，反之亦然。 例如，众所周知，谷歌的搜索引擎算法非常关注新内容，这就是为什么有必要与您网站上的内容保持一致。 这是您可以使用的内容创建方法列表，这些方法可以帮助您进行 SEO。

  • 搜索引擎优化登陆页面很棒有几个原因。 典型的登录页面是为特定原因创建的独立网页。 例如，具有特别优惠促销的销售人员可以创建一个登录页面来宣传优惠。 但是，优化的 SEO 登陆页面适用于搜索引擎。 这意味着着陆页上的某些功能会吸引搜索引擎算法，这些算法决定了对搜索者的价值。
  • 专用博客：另一种不断创建内容的方法是维护博客。 无论您的网站是关于什么的，许多所有者都发现拥有一个博客功能，您可以在其中定期发布具有关键字的相关和新内容，这是在搜索引擎上排名的最佳方式之一。 您创建的内容和关键字越多，您开始在搜索引擎上排名并吸引访问您网站的访问者就越多。 通过共享常规内容，您可以在持续创建内容的前三个月内捕获流量并增长 100% 以上。
  • 评论和推荐是另一种吸引流量到您网站的方式。 它们是您网站的用户提供的内容。 收集推荐和评论是在您的网站上捕获关键字并吸引新访问者的一种形式。 例如，“这是顶级生活方式博客，我很高兴找到它……”这样的评论抓住了这个市场中两个排名靠前的关键词； 顶级生活方式博客和生活方式。 该算法还喜欢评论和推荐； 因此，您从访问者或客户那里收集的评论越多越好。

  • 网站优化：在建立网站时，您应该针对搜索引擎优化网站。 以谷歌搜索引擎为例，他们的算法优先级是显示匹配或最接近搜索查询的结果。 这就是为什么您必须在您的网站上优化某些关键区域的原因，它们包括：

     ** Title Tags** appear as a clickable headline for the search result when displayed as a search snippet on a search engine results page (SERP). The significance is why it should have a focus keyword. Here's a look at TechCabal's title tag - “**Leading Africa's tech conversation”**

    标题标签或标题标签将网页上的标题和副标题与页面内容的其余部分区分开来。 它们也是 HTML 标记，代表介绍性内容及其显示方式。 标题标签对于导航和可读性至关重要，用于改善网页的 SEO。 它们按重要性排序，从 H1 到 H6 文本格式，其中 H1 和 H2 是搜索引擎算法最重要的。 对话”在他们的标题标签中出现。

页面副本与您网站的顶级页面（如主页和关于我们页面）上的网站副本相同。 在这些页面上创建内容时，请确保适度使用关键字。 你的文案应该是有吸引力的，并且应该能够保留

用户连接到页面。

它是互联网上的一个地址，应该尽可能简单。 您的 URL 还可以作为突出关键字的机会，应使用小写字母

用连字符分隔的定义关键字。

这是来自 tech cabal 网站的页面示例：

 **Images** also count when you think of improving your SEO. It is important to use an alternative text(Alt text) on every picture you upload to your site. This is what appears when an image does not load, letting the user know what the image is. These tags help to give context when you use the image search on Google.

此外，在搜索引擎上，您可以使用图像和视频进行搜索。 这是始终正确标记它们的另一个原因。

 **Meta Description** provides a summary of a webpage. This HTML element is displayed as a part of the search engine results page (SERP) snippet and is used to give a user some context as to how the page relates to their query. 谷歌我的商家
此 Google 产品是免费的商家信息，可让您提供您的商家详细信息。 在此服务上创建您的企业资料是免费的，因为此网络工具的目标是帮助本地企业在谷歌搜索引擎上获得更高的知名度。

除了在 Google 我的业务工具上列出您的业务外，您还可以轻松地与目标受众建立联系，不仅是在搜索上，而且通过 Google 地图。 这样，当人们搜索与您提供的产品或服务相关的字词时，您的详细信息就会显示在 Google 搜索引擎的右侧。 但是你的网站呢？ 该工具如何帮助增加流量？

  • 该列表显示访问者如何访问您的网站，其中包含指向您网站的链接。

  • 当人们使用 Google 地图搜索附近的商家时，更有可能出现本地商家的列表； 如果您经营一家小型企业，这是有益的。

优化的“Google 我的商家”网站将为您的业务提供帮助。 但是，获得良好结果的关键是完善您的个人资料并确保其得到全面优化。

电子邮件营销是一种直接营销，涉及向现有订阅者或潜在客户列表发送特定消息。 它使用个性化的群发电子邮件来宣传特别优惠、分享新产品发布或与订阅者建立关系以产生兴趣。 电子邮件营销的潜力是无限的，为您的网站带来流量也是其中的一部分。 如果您一直在通过联系表格、弹出式表格、注册表单或任何其他媒体收集电子邮件地址，那么发送时事通讯是一个不错的起点。

您还可以分享有关您的产品或服务的其他事件或新闻。 您应始终确保号召性用语仍然是“访问我们的网站”。 另外，请记住，电子邮件营销应该适度，因为人们可以轻松地取消订阅您的电子邮件。

电子邮件营销仍然是接触客户和潜在客户的最佳方式之一。 来宾发帖
最好的搜索引擎优化实践是访客发帖，也称为访客博客。 博客文章永远存在于网络上，只要内容仍然相关且可搜索，尤其是常青内容，就会保持相关性。

访客发帖是向另一个个人博客或网站投稿，以建立反向链接、曝光率和权威。 反向链接很重要，因为具有多个反向链接的页面往往具有更高的有机搜索。 就上下文而言，反向链接是从一个网站到另一个网站的链接，并且已被证明是提高搜索排名和促进有关您网站的对话的可靠方法。

访客发帖时，您应该注意那些允许您定位用户正在搜索的关键字的文章，这会为您的网站带来更多流量。

移动优化
访问者从移动设备访问您的网站以获得引人入胜的体验的过程称为移动搜索引擎优化 [Mobile SEO]。

移动优化是一个涉及重新设计网站以提高响应能力的过程。 大多数网站访问者使用他们的手机访问网络，这就是为什么移动优化已成为推动任何网站流量不可或缺的一部分。 优化网站的主要功能包括： 压缩图像以获得更快的加载时间，没有弹出窗口或至少易于关闭弹出窗口，简单，整洁和响应式设计。

移动搜索引擎优化总是考虑用户浏览网站的体验。 谷歌搜索引擎非常重视移动优化，如果您的网站没有经过移动优化，可能不会在搜索结果的第一页对您进行排名。

结论
上面列出的方法是您今天可以应用于网站的不同策略，以帮助您在不增加成本的情况下增加流量。 如果您目前正在建立一个网站或已经有一个网站但难以获得网站访问量，那么遵循这些推荐的步骤将大大帮助您增加网站访问量。

One big challenge that people who own a website face is driving traffic to their websites. The number of visitors you have on your website translates differently depending on the aim of the website, it could be that the goal of the website is to increase sales, generate quality leads, create opportunities, promote brand awareness, generate more revenue, or reach like-minded people and build a community, the reasons are limitless.

Therefore it is vital to drive traffic to your website, you can have the most attractive website, however, if you don't have website traffic, it's just an empty shell and defeats the purpose for creating it in the first place. There are several ways to drive traffic to your website, and they include; using social media, referral sites, paid search, organic search, email newsletters, direct traffic, and others.

Traffic to any website improves visibility and engagement, and the goal is to drive quality visitors to your site that are likely going to convert into customers. In this article, we’ll focus on different effective ways to drive traffic to your website for free. Let's look at how you can get discovered by utilizing free channels:

Search Engine Optimization
Search engines like Google, Yahoo, and MSN are used to gain website traffic. These search engines are designed to collect information based on search queries submitted by internet users. The results presented are called SERPs (search engine results pages).

On the search engine, queries are in a line of results, usually according to ranking. While the search engine’s algorithm is unique and uses varying and complex algorithms to generate search results, a common theme of these algorithms is that they take key elements of a web page like the page title, description tag, the URL, content, and keyword density of a webpage and thus put that page where it ranks on SERPs. With hundreds of websites and new web pages being created daily, among the top two ways to drive traffic to your site is through Search Engine Optimization [SEO].

SEO is one of the prime ways to increase website traffic for free. Here are three ways you can improve your SEO ranking on any of the search engines:

  1. Keywords: The key to on-page SEO is Keyword Strategy. A keyword is a word that carries great significance. For example, if your website is about healthy living, words like fitness, wholesomeness, and regimen are keywords that should appear strategically across your website. These are placements made across the pages on your website. When there's a query, the search engine interacts with the contents on your website, and they appear as SERPs. This is why content creation is our next point.

  2. SEO Content: SEO and Content Creation go hand in hand. SEO cannot work without content, and vice versa. For example, Google's search engine algorithm has been known to focus a lot on new content, and this is why it's necessary to be consistent with the content on your websites. Here's a list of content creation methods you can use that would help you with your SEO.

  • SEO Landing pages are great for several reasons. A typical landing page is a standalone webpage created for a specific reason. For example, a salesperson with a special offer promo can create a landing page to promote the offer. However, optimized SEO landing pages are for the search engine. This means that some features on the landing page appeal to search engine algorithms, which decide the value to searchers.
  • Dedicated Blog: Another way to constantly create content is by maintaining a Blog. Whatever your website is about, many owners have found that having a blog feature where you regularly post relevant and new content that features keywords is one of the best ways to rank on search engines. The more content and keywords you create, the more you begin to rank on the search engines and capture visitors to your website. By sharing regular content, you can capture traffic and grow by over 100% within your first three months of creating content consistently.
  • Reviews & Testimonials are another way to attract traffic to your website. They are content provided by users of your website. Gathering testimonials and reviews are a form of capturing keywords on your website and attracting new visitors. For example, "This is the top lifestyle blog, and I am glad I found it..." A review like this captures two high-ranking keywords in this market; Top lifestyle blog and Lifestyle. The algorithm also loves reviews and testimonials; therefore, the more you can gather reviews from your visitors or customers, the better.

  • Site Optimization: When building a website, you should optimize the site for search engines. Using the Google search engine as an example, their algorithm priority is to show results that match or most closely match the search query. This is why there are certain critical areas you must optimize on your website and they include:

They rank in order of importance, from H1 to H6 text formatting, where H1 and H2 are the most essential for search engines algorithm. Tech Cabals H1 also includes the keyword “Leading Africa’s tech

SEO always seems like work at first. However, with little time and effort, your website traffic would leap once you optimize your website for SEO, use keywords and create regular content. To track progress, you can benchmark organic traffic and keywords on your website before implementing these SEO strategies. You can check back between 2-4 months of traffic growth from your SEO efforts.

Google My Business
This product from Google is a free business listing that allows you to provide your business details. It is free to create your business profile on this service, as this web tool's goal is to help local businesses attain higher visibility on the google search engine.

Apart from listing your business on the Google my business tool, you can easily connect with your target audience, not just on search but through Google Maps. This way, when people search for terms related to the product or service you offer, your details show up on the right side of Google’s search engine. But what about your website? And how does this tool help drive traffic?

  • The listing shows visitors how to visit your website with a link to your website included.
  • It improves your website search and also improves your local SEO.
  • Listing for a local business is more likely to appear when people search for a nearby business using Google Maps; this is beneficial if you run a small business.

An optimized Google My Business website will help your business. However, the key to getting good results is to complete your profile and make sure it is fully optimized.

*Email Marketing *
Email marketing is a type of direct marketing that involves sending out specific messages to a list of already existing subscribers or potential clients. It uses personalized mass emails to promote special offers, share new product releases, or build relationships with subscribers to create interest. The potential of email marketing is limitless, and driving traffic to your website is also a part of it. If you have been collecting email addresses through contact forms, pop-up forms, sign-up forms, or any other medium, sending out newsletters is a great place to start.

You can also share other events or news about your product or service. You should always make sure that the call to action remains “visit our website.” Also, remember that email marketing should be moderate because people can easily unsubscribe from your emails.

Email marketing is still one of the best ways to reach clients and potential clients. Some examples of email marketing companies that run a free package are Mailchimp, Hubspot, and Omnisend.

Guest Posting
Among the best Search Engine Optimization practices is Guest Posting also referred to as guest blogging. Blog posts live perpetually on the web and remain relevant as long as the content is still relevant and searchable, especially the evergreen content.

Guest posting is contributing a post to another personal blog or website to build backlinks, exposure, and authority. Backlinks are significant as pages with several backlinks tend to have a higher organic search. For context, backlinks are links from one website to another website and have proven to be a surefire way to improve your search ranking and boost conversations about your website.

When guest posting, you should pay attention to articles that allow you to target the keywords your users are searching for, which drives more traffic to your website.

Mobile Optimization
A 2021 statistic stated that mobile devices generated 54.8% of global website traffic. The process of visitors accessing your website from mobile devices to have an engaging experience is known as Mobile Search Engine Optimization [Mobile SEO].

Mobile optimization is a process that involves redesigning a website to improve responsiveness. Most website visitors use their mobile phones to access the web and this is why mobile optimization has become an integral part of driving traffic to any website. The key features of an optimized site include; compressed images for faster load times, no pop-ups or at least easy to close pop-ups, simple, uncluttered, and responsive design.

Mobile SEO always considers the user’s experience of navigating the website. Google search engine takes mobile optimization seriously and may not rank you on the first page of the search results if your website is not mobile optimized.

Conclusion
The ways outlined above are different strategies you can apply to websites today to help you increase traffic at no additional cost. If you are currently in the middle of building a website or already have one but struggle to get traffic to your website, following these recommended steps will considerably help you drive traffic to your website.

Paige Niedringhaus - May 15

Items.Title: go-retry - 引入允许您从进程中间重试的 Go 包 | go-retry - Introduction of a Go package that allows you to retry from the middle of a process
Items.Description:
这是 2021 年 11 月在zenn.dev上最初以日语发表的一篇文章的翻译。

为什么我开发它

由于有时我们想一步一步地执行通信、安装等一系列过程，并在每一步重试，我们决定将重试功能泛化，并将其分离为一个包。

起初，实施只是重试，直到现在取消上下文，但正如预期的那样，我们现在正在实施指数退避和最大重试次数等最低要求。

用法

要使用这个包，只需在retry.DoWithContext中设置retry.DurationFunc ，它设置重试间隔，然后为您希望执行的过程的每个步骤传递一个函数。

 package main import ( "context" "log" "github.com/kenkyu392/go-retry" ) func main () { // Prepare context for cancellation. ctx , cancel := context . WithCancel ( context . Background ()) defer cancel () errs := retry . DoWithContext ( ctx , // Retry 5 times while doing Exponential Backoff. retry . ExponentialBackoff ( 5 ), // Separate the process you want to execute into functions // for each step and pass them as arguments. func ( ctx context . Context ) error { // If something occurs that cannot be retried, // the process is canceled with retry.Canceled. if failed { return retry . Canceled } return nil }, // If the previous step is successful, // proceed to the next step. func ( ctx context . Context ) error { return nil }, ) // Check all errors that occurred during execution. for _ , err := range errs { log . Print ( err ) } }

什么是指数退避？

简单来说，就是根据进程失败的次数成倍增加重试间隔的算法。 如果您查看细节，您可以找到一个很好的解释，即延长重试间隔的必要性，但我认为它通常是为了“避免处理冲突”或“减少负载”而实现的。

如何调整重试间隔

目前这个包实现了标准的retry.ExponentialBackoff和 retry.Duration]( https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/kenkyu392/go-retry#Duration )。

让重试间隔调整成为一个函数的原因部分是因为我们喜欢让它成为一个简单的可定制实现，但主要是因为指数退避有许多不同的实现。 例如，当前的retry.ExponentialBackoff实现是通过使用随机值的算法实现的，例如200ms + (20ms ~ 100ms) 。 通过如下实现您自己的函数，可以在不使用随机值的情况下使用指数退避。

 func ExponentialBackoff ( maxRetries int ) retry . DurationFunc { return func ( retries int ) time . Duration { if maxRetries >= 0 && maxRetries < retries { return - 1 } return time . Duration ( math . Pow ( 2 , float64 ( retries )) * 100 ) * time . Millisecond } }

まとめ

这个包的设计和实现是为了与通信过程、安装过程、数据库等以及具有交互界面的 CLI 一起使用。 它被设计成易于集成到各个地方，所以如果你喜欢，请使用它。

我也欢迎任何关于retry.DurationFunc和其他贡献的补充或建议。



原文:

This is a translation of an article originally published in Japanese on zenn.dev in November 2021.

Why I developed it

Since there were times when we wanted to execute a series of processes such as communication and installation step by step and retry at each step, we decided to generalize the retry function and separate it as a package.

https://github.com/kenkyu392/go-retry

At first, the implementation was just to retry until the context is canceled for now, but as expected, we are now implementing minimum requirements such as Exponential Backoff and maximum retry count.

Usage

To use this package, simply set retry.DurationFunc, which sets the retry interval, in retry.DoWithContext, and then pass a function for each step of the process you wish to execute.

package main import ( "context" "log" "github.com/kenkyu392/go-retry"
) func main() { // Prepare context for cancellation. ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background()) defer cancel() errs := retry.DoWithContext(ctx, // Retry 5 times while doing Exponential Backoff. retry.ExponentialBackoff(5), // Separate the process you want to execute into functions // for each step and pass them as arguments. func(ctx context.Context) error { // If something occurs that cannot be retried, // the process is canceled with retry.Canceled. if failed { return retry.Canceled } return nil }, // If the previous step is successful, // proceed to the next step. func(ctx context.Context) error { return nil }, ) // Check all errors that occurred during execution. for _, err := range errs { log.Print(err) }
}

What is Exponential Backoff?

Simply explained, it is an algorithm that exponentially increases the retry interval according to the number of process failures. You can find a good explanation of the necessity of lengthening the retry interval if you look into the details, but I think it is often implemented for "avoiding processing conflicts" or "reducing the load.

https://cloud.google.com/iot/docs/how-tos/exponential-backoff

https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/api-retries.html

How to adjust retry interval

The retry.DurationFunc interface can be used to implement any retry interval algorithm. Currently this package implements the standard retry.ExponentialBackoff and retry.Duration](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/kenkyu392/go-retry#Duration).

The reason for making the retry interval adjustment a function is partly because we like to make it a simple customizable implementation, but mostly because there are many different implementations of Exponential Backoff. For example, the current implementation of retry.ExponentialBackoff is implemented with an algorithm using random values such as 200ms + (20ms ~ 100ms). Exponential Backoff can be used without using random values by implementing your own function as follows.

func ExponentialBackoff(maxRetries int) retry.DurationFunc { return func(retries int) time.Duration { if maxRetries >= 0 && maxRetries < retries { return -1 } return time.Duration(math.Pow(2, float64(retries))*100)*time.Millisecond }
}

まとめ

This package is designed and implemented for use with communication processes, installation processes, databases, etc., as well as CLI with interactive interfaces. It is designed to be easy to integrate into various places, so please use it if you like.

I would also welcome any additions or suggestions for retry.DurationFunc and other contributions.

Items.Title: SOLID: O conceito por trás do código | SOLID: O conceito por trás do código
Items.Description:
一个 seguir será detalhado cada um desses princípios acima。

作为类 não devemdepender de coisas de que não precisam。

类 concretas são toleráveis pois sabe-se que elas não irão mudar。

结论

参考文献

  • 第一个。 编。 美国：Prentice Hall 出版社，2017 年。ISBN 0134494164。
  • 迈耶，伯特兰。 面向对象的软件构建。 新泽西州上马鞍河：Prentice Hall，1997 年。ISBN 978-0-13-629155-8。


原文:

Muitas empresas pedem em suas posições de desenvolvimento que os profissionais tenham conhecimento de SOLID. No entanto, muitos acreditam que é apenas um conjunto de práticas durante o desenvolvimento, mas não conhecendo de fato a teoria por trás.

Os princípios S.O.L.I.D., um acrônimo com as iniciais dos princípios abordados a seguir, tem como finalidade organizar as funções e estruturas de dados de forma a facilitar o desenvolvimento e evolução de software. O objetivo desses princípios é criar códigos que tolerem mudanças, sejam fáceis de entender e que sejam a base de componentes que possam ser usados em diversos sistemas.

S - Single Responsiblity Principle (SRP, ou Princípio da Responsabilidade Única em português)
O - Open-Closed Principle (OCP, ou Princípio Aberto-Fechado em português)
L - Liskov Substitution Principle (LSP, ou Princípio da substituição de Liskov em português)
I - Interface Segregation Principle (ISP, ou Princípio da Segregação da Interface em português)
D - Dependency Inversion Principle (DIP, ou Princípio da inversão da dependência em português)

A seguir será detalhado cada um desses princípios acima.

SRP: O Princípio da Responsabilidade Única

Esse princípio mostra que uma classe deve ser especializada em um único assunto e ter apenas uma responsabilidade. Em outras palavras, a classe deve ter uma única ação para executar.

MARTIN (2017) reformula a descrição comumente usada, de que um módulo deve ter apenas uma razão para mudar, e disserta que o SRP deve ser descrito como um módulo que deve ser responsável por apenas um ator. Esse ator é a representação de um usuário ou stakeholder que são a ”razão para mudar” da definição inicial. O termo módulo pode ser definido como o arquivo-fonte ou um conjunto coeso de funções e estrutura de dados.

A violação desse princípio pode fazer com que uma classe assuma responsabilidades que não são suas, aumentar o nível de acoplamento, gerar dificuldades na implementação de testes automatizados e tornar difícil o reaproveitamento do código.

OCP: O Princípio Aberto/Fechado

Esse princípio foi criado por Bertrand MEYER (1997) e afirma que um software deve ser aberto para extensão, mas fechado para modificação. Ou seja, quando novos recursos precisam ser adicionados no sistema ele deve ser estendido e não alterar o código fonte original.

Segundo MARTIN (2017), o OCP é uma das forças motrizes na arquitetura de sistemas, sendo este princípio o mais importante da orientação a objetos. Para que seja possível cumprir com o objetivo de o sistema ser fácil sem que essa mudança cause um impacto grande é preciso dividir o sistemas em componentes. Esses componentes são organizados em uma hierarquia de dependências, de forma a proteger os componentes de alto nível das mudanças realizadas em componentes de níveis inferiores.

LSP: O Princípio de Substituição de Liskov

O Princípio da Substituição de Liskov foi definido por Barbara Liskov e disserta sobre usar como base a propriedade de substituição. Essa propriedade é descrita pela demonstração:

se, para cada objeto o1 de tipo S, houver um objeto o2 de tipo T, de modo que, para todos os programas P definidos em termos de T, o comportamento de P não seja modificado quando o1 for substituído por o2, então S é um subtipo de T. (MEYER, 1997).

Em outras palavras, o LSP diz que se S é um subtipo de T, então os objetos do tipo T em um programa podem ser substituídos pelos objetos de tipo S sem a necessidade de alterar as propriedades desse sistema.

O LSP se transformou em um princípio mais amplo de padrão de projeto, podendo ser aplicado em interfaces e implementações. Esse princípio nos diz que as implementações devem obedecer a algum tipo de contrato. A aplicação do LSP é importante, pois os usuários precisam de interfaces bem definidas e dessa capacidade de substituição de implementação das interfaces. Esse princípio deve ser aplicado à arquitetura, dado que uma violação na capacidade de substituição pode contaminar a arquitetura do sistema com uma grande quantidade de mecanismos extras (MARTIN, 2017). Alguns exemplos do que pode acontecer ao violar esse princípio são:

  • lançar uma exceção inesperada;
  • sobrescrever ou implementar um método que não faz nada;
  • retornar valores de tipos diferentes da classe base.

ISP: O Princípio da Segregação de Interface

Esse princípio mostra que uma classe não deve ser forçada a implementar interfaces e métodos que não vá utilizar, ou seja, é melhor criar interfaces mais específicas do que ter uma grande interface genérica. As classes não devem depender de coisas de que não precisam.

Utiliza-se esse princípio na arquitetura poder ser prejudicial depender de módulos com muitos elementos desnecessários. Isso é válido tanto em níveis arquiteturais mais altos quanto mais baixos, como por exemplo as dependências de código fonte podem forçar de forma desnecessária a recompilação e reimplantação.

DIP: O Princípio da Inversão de Dependências

Sobre o Princípio de Inversão de Dependências, MARTIN (2017) expõe que os sistemas mais flexíveis são aqueles nos quais as dependências de código-fonte se referem apenas a abstrações e não a itens concretos.

No geral, fala que nenhuma classe ou função deve se preocupar com a forma como uma determinada coisa é feita. Algumas classes, como por exemplo a classe String, são concretas, então não seria interessante forçá-las a ser abstratas. Entretanto, essas classes são muito estáveis de forma que as mudanças nelas são muito raras e controladas, logo não é necessário se preocupar com essas mudanças. Classes concretas são toleráveis pois sabe-se que elas não irão mudar.

Aplicando à Arquitetura Limpa, os módulos de alto nível não devem depender de módulos de baixo nível, os dois devem depender da abstração. As abstrações não devem depender de detalhes e sim os detalhes que devem depender das abstrações.

Conclusão

SOLID não é apenas mais uma linha nos requisitos das empresas ao buscar por profissionais, é um conjunto essencial de boas práticas.

Quando esses princípios são aplicados nos sistemas, eles tendem a ser mais bem estruturados, coesos e que escalam de maneira mais harmônica - sendo eles fundamentais para a Arquitetura Limpa.

Todo bom profissional deve buscar se aprofundar nos princípios SOLID, aplicá-los em seu dia a dia e disseminá-los em seus times. Boas práticas devem ser difundidas e aplicadas.

Referências

  • MARTIN, Robert C. Clean Architecture: A Craftsman’s Guide to Software Structure and Design. 1st. ed. USA: Prentice Hall Press, 2017. ISBN 0134494164.
  • MEYER, Bertrand. Object-Oriented Software Construction. 2. ed. Upper Saddle River, NJ: Prentice Hall, 1997. ISBN 978-0-13-629155-8.

Items.Title: 了解 TypeScript (NestJS) 中的数据传输对象 (DTO) 和数据验证 | Understanding Data Transfer Objects (DTO) and Data Validation in TypeScript (NestJS)
Items.Description:
数据传输对象 (DTO) 是 NestJS 应用程序中数据验证的基础。 在本出版物中，您将深入了解数据传输对象，讨论验证机制、身份验证模型以及您需要了解的有关数据传输对象的所有其他信息。

先决条件

本教程不是以初学者为主题的。 如果您刚刚开始使用 NestJS，请阅读这篇文章。

您需要满足这些要求才能完全理解本文。

  • 以前使用 MongoDB 创建 NestJS 应用程序的经验。
  • 使用 PostMan（推荐）或任何其他 API 测试工具的能力。
  • 满足这些要求后，我们就可以开始了。

    什么是数据传输对象？

    数据传输对象，通常称为 DTO，是用于验证数据并定义发送到 Nest 应用程序的数据结构的对象。 接口用于类型检查和结构定义。

  • 接口在编译过程中会消失，因为它是 TypeScript 原生的，并且在 JavaScript 中不存在。
  • 因此，它在编译后仍然存在。

单独的 DTO 只能进行类型检查和结构定义。 要使用 DTO 运行数据验证，您需要使用 NestJS ValidationPipe 。

验证机制

管道用于验证 NestJS 中的数据。 对通过管道的数据进行评估，如果通过验证测试，则原样返回； 否则，将引发错误。

简单用户身份验证模型中的 DTO

用户身份验证模型是揭开 DTO 神秘面纱的完美示例。 这是因为它需要多个级别的数据验证。 让我们创建一个并探索 DTO 和ValidationPipe来验证进入我们应用程序的所有形式的数据。

设置开发环境

要设置您的开发环境：

  • 使用 Nest CLI 生成一个新项目，
  • 使用 CLI 生成Module 、 Service和Controller ，
  • 安装Mongoose并将您的应用程序连接到数据库，
  • 创建您的架构文件夹并定义Schema 。

典型的用户身份验证模式应如下所示：

 import { Prop , Schema , SchemaFactory } from ' @nestjs/mongoose ' ; import { Document } from ' mongoose ' ; export type UserDocument = User & Document ; @ Schema () export class User { @ Prop ({ required : true }) fullName : string ; @ Prop ({ required : true }) email : string ; @ Prop ({ required : true }) password : string ; } export const UserSchema = SchemaFactory . createForClass ( User );

将fullName 、 email和password作为必需属性。

  • 在模块内创建一个文件夹并将其命名为dto 。 这是您的 dto 类将被存储和导出的地方。
  • 在dto文件夹中创建一个文件并将其命名为user.dto.ts 。

上面是一个基本 DTO 的结构。

此结构仅对类型检查有用，它没有数据验证属性。

实施数据验证

添加验证功能； 请按照以下步骤操作；

  • 在你的main.ts里面，
  • 从'@nestjs/common'导入{ValidationPipe} ，
  • 在bootstrap函数内部和app常量的正下方，调用app上的useGlobalPipes方法并传入new ValidationPipe()作为参数。
 import { NestFactory } from ' @nestjs/core ' ; import { AppModule } from ' ./app.module ' ; import { ValidationPipe } from ' @nestjs/common ' ; import * as dotenv from ' dotenv ' ; dotenv . config (); async function bootstrap () { const app = await NestFactory . create ( AppModule ); app . useGlobalPipes ( new ValidationPipe ()); await app . listen ( process . env . PORT ); } bootstrap ();

这将启用自动验证并确保所有端点都不会收到不正确的数据。 验证管道接受一个选项对象作为参数，您可以在官方文档中找到更多信息。

  • 通过运行以下命令安装class-validator和class-transformer包：
 npm i -- save class - validator class - transformer

在您的user.dto.ts文件中，从class-validator导入以下查询：

  • 它接受一个选项对象作为参数。
  • 它接受一个选项对象作为参数。
  • 它接受一个选项对象作为参数。
  • 第一个是minLength ，然后是maxLength ，最后是选项对象。

以上将用作装饰器来验证它们各自的字段。 在你的 DTO 中实现它，

 import { IsNotEmpty , IsEmail , Length , IsString } from ' class-validator ' ; export class newUserDto { @ IsNotEmpty ({ message : ' Please Enter Full Name ' }) @ IsString ({ message : ' Please Enter Valid Name ' }) fullName : string ; @ IsEmail ({ message : ' Please Enter a Valid Email ' }) email : string ; @ Length ( 6 , 50 , { message : ' Password length Must be between 6 and 50 charcters ' , }) password : string ; }

密码字段只有一个Length验证，但在生产中，您可能需要添加更多字段以确保密码更安全。

这些新的验证字段可能包括检查密码是否包含大写、小写和数字。

这可以通过多种方式完成，包括：

  • 填充更多验证装饰器，就像您在fullName字段中所做的那样。
  • 使用@Matches验证器并将检查您的要求的正则表达式作为参数传递。

但最简单的方法是创建一个自定义验证器来满足您的规范。 您可以在此处了解有关自定义装饰器的更多信息。

发布和提交请求

通过向您的应用程序发出一些POST和PUT请求来测试数据验证。 但在此之前，以纯文本形式存储密码是一种不好的做法。 因此，在将它们存储到数据库之前先对它们进行哈希处理；

散列密码

您可以使用名为bcrypt的包或 NodeJS 加密模块对密码进行哈希处理。 本出版物涵盖bcrypt 。 安装bcrypt运行

npm i bcrypt npm i -D @types/bcrypt

创建一个utils文件夹来存储包含实用程序函数的文件，例如用于散列密码的函数。

为确保用户密码受到保护，您需要在将它们存储到数据库之前对其进行哈希处理。 让我们实现这个

  • 在您的utils文件夹中创建一个文件，该文件将包含您的实用程序函数。
  • 从'bcrypt'导入*作为bcrypt
  • 创建一个函数，接收原始密码并使用bcrypt对其进行哈希处理，然后返回哈希密码。 导出函数。
 import * as bcrypt from ' bcrypt ' ; export async function hashPassword ( textPassword : string ) { const salt = await bcrypt . genSalt (); const hash = await bcrypt . hash ( textPassword , salt ); return hash ; }

有多种使用bcrypt包的方法，您可以在此处找到。

现在你已经有了一个哈希密码的函数，实现你的第一个POST请求。

创建新用户

要使用有效凭据创建新用户，您需要将之前创建的 DTO 导入您的服务。

服务逻辑

  • 创建一个async函数newUser ，它接受一个参数user ，它应该是新用户的数据。 将其类型设置为之前创建的 DTO。
  • 导入hashPassword函数。
  • 在async函数内部，创建一个常量password ，并以user.password作为参数分配等待hashPassword函数的返回值。
  • 返回您自己的userModel版本的新实例作为参数传入包含解构的user和password的对象，并在其上调用save()方法。
 import { Injectable } from ' @nestjs/common ' ; import { Model } from ' mongoose ' ; import { InjectModel } from ' @nestjs/mongoose ' ; import { User , UserDocument } from ' ./schemas/user.schema ' ; import { newUserDto } from ' ./dto/user.dto ' ; import { hashPassword } from ' ./utilis/bcrypt.utils ' ; @ Injectable () export class AuthService { constructor ( @ InjectModel ( User . name ) private userModel : Model < UserDocument > , ) {} async newUser ( user : newUserDto ): Promise < User > { const password = await hashPassword ( user . password ); return await new this . userModel ({ ... user , password }). save (); } }

我们之前设置的 DTO 和验证器会在将用户数据存储到数据库之前不断检查数据是否有效。

实现控制器逻辑，以便您可以使用一些虚拟数据测试 DTO。

控制器逻辑

import { Controller , Body , Post , } from ' @nestjs/common ' ; import { AuthService } from ' ./auth.service ' ; import { newUserDto } from ' ./dto/user.dto ' ; @ Controller ( ' users ' ) export class AuthController { constructor ( private readonly service : AuthService ) {} @ Post ( ' signup ' ) async createUser ( @ Body () user : newUserDto , ): Promise < newUserDto > { return await this . service . newUser ( user ); }

正如您在上面的代码块中看到的， user参数的类型为newUserDto 。 这可以确保进入应用程序的所有数据都与 DTO 匹配，否则会引发错误。

测试端点

通过使用 PostMan 或您首选的测试工具向http://localhost:3000/users/signup发出请求，使用一些虚拟数据测试验证。

 { "fullName" : "Jon Snow" , "email" : "snow@housestark.com" , "password" : "password"
}

上面的 JSON 数据检查所有验证框。 因此，您将收到带有数据的201响应，带有_id属性和散列密码。 复制_id属性，在PUT请求中测试数据验证时需要它。

 { "fullName" : "Arya Stark" , "email" : "aryathefaceless@housestark" , "password" : "password"
}

上面的 JSON 数据具有无效的email属性。 因此，您将收到400响应，其中包含描述错误的消息。 通过将email属性更新为aryathefaceless@housestark.com将其保存到数据库中。

 { "fullName" : "Tyrion Lannister" , "email" : "tyrion@houselannister.com" , "password" : "pass" , "house" : "Lannister"
}

上面的 JSON 数据有 2 个问题，密码太短（少于 6 个字符），还有一个额外的字段house 。

增加password长度并再次发送请求。 您将收到一个包含已处理数据的200请求，但house字段未存储在数据库中，因为它在通过验证管道时已被过滤。

更新用户

实现PUT路由以在需要时更新用户数据。

服务逻辑

async updateUser ( id : string , userData : newUserDto ): Promise < User > { return await this . userModel . findByIdAndUpdate ( id , userData ); }

控制器逻辑

@ Put ( ' :id ' ) async updateuser ( @ Param ( ' id ' ) id : string , @ Body () user : newUserDto , ): Promise < newUserDto > { return this . service . updateUser ( id , user ); }

与POST请求类似，给user一个类型的newUserDto 。 因此，在将所有数据保存到数据库之前，都会对其进行彻底检查。

通过更新存储在数据库中的用户之一来测试此端点。 回想一下，您复制了属于 Jon Snow 的_id 。

因此，使用以下数据向http://localhost:3000/id发出PUT请求；

 { "fullName" : "Jon Snow" , "email" : "snow@housetargaryen.com" , "password" : "password"
}

上面的数据会检查所有验证框，因此它会返回200响应。 如果任何数据字段包含无效数据，它将返回400响应并且PUT请求将失败。

登录用户

如果您无法登录用户，则用户身份验证模型将不完整。为此实现逻辑；

首先，您需要一个bcrypt实用程序函数来比较散列密码和纯文本密码。 像这样，

 export async function validatePassword ( textPassword : string , hash : string ) { const validUser = await bcrypt . compare ( textPassword , hash ); return validUser ; }

然后，您必须为登录数据创建一个新的 DTO。 请注意，没有将验证器添加到password属性。 这是因为它可能会在未来构成安全威胁，因为它会将密码长度范围暴露给恶意用户。

服务逻辑

async loginUser ( loginData : loginUserDto ) { const email = loginData . email ; const user = await this . userModel . findOne ({ email }); if ( user ) { const valid = await validatePassword ( loginData . password , user . password ); if ( valid ) { return user ; } } return new UnauthorizedException ( ' Invalid Credentials ' ); }

您的服务逻辑应该能够，

检查用户是否存在；

  • 如果用户存在，验证密码
  • 如果用户不存在，则抛出异常

验证密码；

  • 如果密码匹配，则返回用户
  • 如果密码不匹配，则抛出异常

控制器逻辑

@ Post ( ' login ' ) async loginUser ( @ Body () loginData : loginUserDto , ) { return await this . service . loginUser ( loginData ); }

您的控制器应该向http://localhost:3000/users/login发出POST请求。

结论

你终于在这篇文章的结尾。 这是您所涵盖的内容的回顾。

  • 什么是 DTO，
  • 使用 bcrypt 制作一个简单的用户认证模型。

这是相当多的，恭喜你走到了这一步。

你可以在Github上找到代码。

快乐编码！



原文:

Data Transfer Objects (DTOs) are the basis of data validation in NestJS applications. DTOs enable various layers of flexible data validation in NestJS.

In this publication, you will take a deep dive into data transfer objects, discussing validation mechanisms, authentication models, and everything else you need to know about data transfer objects.

Prerequisites

This tutorial is not beginner-themed. If you’re just getting started with NestJS, read this article.

You’ll need to meet these requirements to fully understand this article.

  • Prior experience creating NestJS apps with MongoDB.
  • Competence with PostMan (recommended) or any other API testing tool.
  • Basic knowledge of bcrypt or the NodeJS crypto module.

Once you meet these requirements, we can get started.

What is a Data Transfer Object?

A data transfer object, commonly called a DTO, is an object used to validate data and define data structure sent into your Nest applications. DTOs are similar to interfaces, but differ from interfaces in the following ways:

  • Interfaces are used for type-checking and structure definition.
  • A DTO is used for type-checking, structure definition, and data validation.
  • Interfaces disappear during compilation, as it’s native to TypeScript and doesn't exist in JavaScript.
  • DTOs are defined using classes that are supported in native JavaScript. Hence, it remains after compilation.

DTOs alone can only do type-checking and structure definition. To run data validations using DTOs, you’ll need to use the NestJS ValidationPipe.

Validation Mechanisms

Pipes are used to validate data in NestJS. Data passing through a pipe is evaluated, if it passes the validation test, it is returned unmodified; otherwise, an error is thrown.

NestJS has 8 inbuilt pipes, but you’ll be focusing on the ValidationPipe which makes use of the class-validator package, because it abstracts a lot of verbose code and makes it easy to validate data using decorators.

DTOs in a Simple User Authentication Model

A user authentication model is a perfect example to demystify DTOs. This is because it requires multiple levels of data validation. Let’s make one and explore DTOs and the ValidationPipe to validate all forms of data coming into our application.

Setting up Development Environment

To set up your development environment:

  • Generate a new project using the Nest CLI,
  • Generate your Module, Service, and Controller using the CLI,
  • Install Mongoose and connect your application to the database,
  • Create your schema folder and define the Schema.

A typical user-auth schema should look like:

import { Prop, Schema, SchemaFactory } from '@nestjs/mongoose';
import { Document } from 'mongoose'; export type UserDocument = User & Document; @Schema()
export class User { @Prop({ required: true }) fullName: string; @Prop({ required: true }) email: string; @Prop({ required: true }) password: string;
}
export const UserSchema = SchemaFactory.createForClass(User);

Having the fullName, email, and password as required properties.

  • Create a folder inside your module and call it dto. This is where your dto classes will be stored and exported.
  • Create a file inside your dto folder and name it user.dto.ts.

Structure of a DTO

A DTO is a class, so it follows the same syntax a class does.

export class newUserDto { fullName: string; email: string; password: string;
}

Above is the structure of a basic DTO.

This structure is only useful for type-checking, it has no data validation properties.

Implementing Data Validation

To add validation functions; follow the steps below;

  • Inside your main.ts,
  • Import {ValidationPipe} from '@nestjs/common',
  • Inside the bootstrap function and directly below the app constant, call the useGlobalPipes method on app and pass in new ValidationPipe() as an argument. 
import { NestFactory } from '@nestjs/core';
import { AppModule } from './app.module';
import { ValidationPipe } from '@nestjs/common';
import * as dotenv from 'dotenv';
dotenv.config(); async function bootstrap() { const app = await NestFactory.create(AppModule); app.useGlobalPipes(new ValidationPipe()); await app.listen(process.env.PORT);
}
bootstrap();

This will enable auto-validation and ensure that all endpoints are protected from receiving incorrect data. The Validation pipe takes in an options object as an argument, you can find out more about it in the official documentation.

  • Install the class-validator and the class-transformer package by running: 
npm i --save class-validator class-transformer

The class-transformer converts JSON objects into an instance of your DTO class and vice-versa.

The class-validator package has a lot of validation queries, which you can find in their documentation, but you’ll be focusing on a few of them related to user data validation. In your user.dto.ts file, import the following queries from class-validator:

  • IsNotEmpty: This validator checks if a given value is empty. It takes in an object of options as an argument.
  • IsString: This validator checks if a given value is a real string. It takes in an object of options as an argument.
  • IsEmail: This validator checks if a given value is of type email. It takes in an object of options as an argument.
  • Length: This Validator takes in 3 arguments. The first is the minLength, then the maxLength, and finally an object of options.

The above will be used as decorators to validate their respective fields. Implement this in your DTO,

import { IsNotEmpty, IsEmail, Length, IsString } from 'class-validator'; export class newUserDto { @IsNotEmpty({ message: 'Please Enter Full Name' }) @IsString({ message: 'Please Enter Valid Name' }) fullName: string; @IsEmail({ message: 'Please Enter a Valid Email' }) email: string; @Length(6, 50, { message: 'Password length Must be between 6 and 50 charcters', }) password: string;
}

The password field has only a Length validation but in production, you might need to add a few more fields to ensure the password is more secure.

These new validation fields might include checking if the password contains uppercase, lowercase, and numbers.

This can be done in several ways, including:

  • Padding with more validation decorators, like you did in the fullName field.
  • Using the @Matches validator and passing a regular expression that checks for your requirements as an argument.

But the cleanest way to do this would be by creating a custom validator to suit your specifications. You can learn more about custom decorators here.

Post & Put Requests

Test the data validation by making a few POST and PUT requests to your application. But before that, it’s bad practice to store your passwords in plain text. So hash them first before storing them in the database;

Hashing Passwords

You can hash the passwords using a package called bcrypt or the NodeJS crypto module. This publication covers bcrypt. To install bcrypt run

npm i bcrypt
npm i -D @types/bcrypt

Create a utils folder to store files that contain utility functions, like the function you’ll use to hash the passwords.

To ensure that user passwords are protected, you’ll need to hash them before they are stored in the database. Let’s implement this

  • Create a file in your utils folder, this will contain your utility functions.
  • Import * as bcrypt from 'bcrypt'
  • Create a function that takes in the raw password and hashes it using bcrypt then returns the hashed password. Export the function. 
import * as bcrypt from 'bcrypt'; export async function hashPassword(textPassword: string) { const salt = await bcrypt.genSalt(); const hash = await bcrypt.hash(textPassword, salt); return hash;
}

There are different ways to use the bcrypt package, which you can find here.

Now that you have a function to hash our passwords, Implement your first POST request.

Creating a New User

To create a new user with valid credentials, you’ll need to import the DTO you created earlier into your service.

Service Logic

  • Create an async function newUser that takes in a parameter user which should be the data of the new user. Set its type to the DTO created earlier.
  • Import the hashPassword function.
  • Inside the async function, create a constant password, and assign the return value of awaiting the hashPassword function with user.password as an argument.
  • Return a new instance of your own version of my userModel as an argument pass in an object containing a destructured user and the password, and call the save() method on it. 
import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common';
import { Model } from 'mongoose';
import { InjectModel } from '@nestjs/mongoose';
import { User, UserDocument } from './schemas/user.schema';
import { newUserDto } from './dto/user.dto';
import { hashPassword } from './utilis/bcrypt.utils'; @Injectable()
export class AuthService { constructor( @InjectModel(User.name) private userModel: Model<UserDocument>, ) {} async newUser(user: newUserDto): Promise<User> { const password = await hashPassword(user.password); return await new this.userModel({ ...user, password }).save(); }
}

The DTO and the validators we set up earlier will continuously check if the data is valid before storing the user data in the database.

Implement the controller logic so that you can test the DTO with some dummy data.

Controller Logic

import { Controller, Body, Post, } from '@nestjs/common';
import { AuthService } from './auth.service';
import { newUserDto } from './dto/user.dto'; @Controller('users')
export class AuthController { constructor(private readonly service: AuthService) {} @Post('signup') async createUser( @Body() user: newUserDto, ): Promise<newUserDto> { return await this.service.newUser(user); }

As you can see in the code block above, the user parameter has a type of newUserDto. This ensures that all data coming into the application matches the DTO, else it throws an error.

Testing Endpoints

Test the validation with some dummy data by making requests to http://localhost:3000/users/signup using PostMan or your preferred testing tool.

{ "fullName":"Jon Snow", "email":"snow@housestark.com", "password":"password"
}

The JSON data above checks all validation boxes. Hence, you’ll get a 201 response with data, with an _id property and a hashed password. Copy the _id property, you’ll need it when testing data validation in PUT requests.

{ "fullName":"Arya Stark", "email":"aryathefaceless@housestark", "password":"password"
}

The JSON data above has an invalid email property. Hence, you’ll get a 400 response with a message that describes the error. Save it to the database by updating the email property to aryathefaceless@housestark.com.

{ "fullName":"Tyrion Lannister", "email":"tyrion@houselannister.com", "password":"pass", "house":"Lannister"
}

The JSON data above has 2 problems, the password is too short (less than 6 characters) and there is an extra field house.

Increase the length of the password and send the request again. You’ll get a 200 request with the processed data, but the house field isn’t stored in the database because it was filtered when it was passed through the validation pipe.

Updating a User

Implement a PUT route to update user data when required.

Service Logic

async updateUser(id: string, userData: newUserDto): Promise<User> { return await this.userModel.findByIdAndUpdate(id, userData); }

Controller Logic

@Put(':id') async updateuser( @Param('id') id: string, @Body() user: newUserDto, ): Promise<newUserDto> { return this.service.updateUser(id, user); }

Similar to the POST request, the user is given a type of newUserDto. Hence, all data is checked thoroughly before it is saved in the database.

Test this endpoint by updating one of the users stored in your database. Recall that you copied the _id belonging to Jon Snow.

So make a PUT request to http://localhost:3000/id with the following data;

{ "fullName":"Jon Snow", "email":"snow@housetargaryen.com", "password":"password"
}

The data above checks all validation boxes, so it would return a 200 response. If any of the data fields contain invalid data, it would return a 400 response and the PUT request would fail.

Logging Users in

A user-authentication model wouldn't be complete if you couldn't log users in. To implement the logic for that;

Firstly, you’ll need a bcrypt utility function that will compare the hashed and plain text passwords. Like so,

export async function validatePassword(textPassword: string, hash: string) { const validUser = await bcrypt.compare(textPassword, hash); return validUser;
}

Then, you’ll have to create a new DTO for the login data. The DTO is similar to the newUserDto but without the fullName property.

import { IsEmail } from 'class-validator'; export class loginUserDto { @IsEmail({ message: 'Please Enter a Valid Email' }) email: string; password: string;
}

Notice that no validator was added to the password property. This is because it could pose a security threat in the future, as it exposes the password length range to malicious users.

Service Logic

async loginUser(loginData: loginUserDto) { const email = loginData.email; const user = await this.userModel.findOne({ email }); if (user) { const valid = await validatePassword(loginData.password, user.password); if (valid) { return user; } } return new UnauthorizedException('Invalid Credentials'); }

Your service logic should be able to,

Check if the user exists;

  • If the user exists, validate the passwords
  • If the user doesn't exist, throw an exception

Validate Passwords;

  • If the passwords match, return the user
  • If the passwords don’t match, throw an exception

Controller Logic

@Post('login') async loginUser( @Body() loginData: loginUserDto, ) { return await this.service.loginUser(loginData); }

Your controller should make the POST request to http://localhost:3000/users/login.

Conclusion

You’re finally at the end of this article. Here’s a recap what you’ve covered.

  • What a DTO is,
  • Differences between a DTO and an interface,
  • Structure of a DTO,
  • NestJS validation mechanism,
  • Making a simple user-authentication model with bcrypt.

That’s quite a lot, congratulations on making it this far.

You can find the code on Github.

Happy Coding!

Items.Title: Tailwind CSS 自定义摘要 | Tailwind CSS Customization Summary
Items.Description:
使用 tailwindcss 3.0.23

添加颜色主题

在tailwind中，你可以使用bg-white为白色背景颜色， text-white为白色文本等，但你可以在white部分使用自己的颜色。

允许指定背景颜色foo和文本颜色bar-buz 。

 <div class= "bg-foo text-bar-buz" > ・・・</div>

使用自定义字体

读取字体文件

或加载css

 @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Dancing+Script&display=swap') ;

指定字体名称和字体系列

可以使用font-fancy 。

可以在这篇文章中找到添加谷歌字体的说明

把班级放在一起

可以使用@apply创建一个总结tailwind类的类。

在这种情况下base < components < utilities 。

 @layer base { h1 { @apply text-2xl ; } } @layer components { h1 { @apply text-4xl ; } } @layer utilities { h1 { @apply text-5xl ; } }

这些用途引自翻译的文件

根据

基础层用于重置规则或应用于普通 HTML 元素的默认样式。

组件

组件层适用于您希望能够使用实用程序覆盖的基于类的样式。

公用事业

实用程序层适用于小型、单一用途的类，它们应始终优先于任何其他样式。

切换亮/暗模式

如何判断是否处于暗模式

在这种情况下，它作为暗模式运行

<html class= "dark" > ・・・<div class= "bg-white dark:bg-black" > ・・・</div> </html>

动态添加和删除dark类以在暗模式和亮模式之间切换

切换主题

在此示例中创建颜色primary和primary-text

用css设置颜色

/* default theme */ :root { --color-primary : #ffffff ; --color-primary-text : #000000 ; } /* alternate-theme */ .custom-theme { --color-primary : #000000 ; --color-primary-text : #ffffff ; }

通过指定主题的类名来应用

<div class= "custom-theme" > <button class= "bg-primary text-primary-text" > Button </button> </div>

例如，如果使主题的类名动态化，则可以为每个用户切换应用程序的主题。

你可以在这里查看。
https://github.com/tailwindlabs/tailwindcss/blob/master/stubs/defaultConfig.stub.js https://github.com/tailwindlabs/tailwindcss/blob/master/stubs/defaultConfig.stub.js



原文:

Worked with tailwindcss 3.0.23

Add color theme

In tailwind, you can use bg-white for white background color, text-white for white text, etc., but you can use your own color in the white part.

tailwind.config.js

module.exports = { ・・・ theme: { extend: { colors: { "foo": "#f97316", "bar": { "buz": "#65C18C", ・・・ }, }, }, }, ・・・
}

Allows specifying background color foo and text color bar-buz.

<div class="bg-foo text-bar-buz">　・・・</div>

Use custom fonts

Read font file

html

<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Dancing+Script&display=swap" rel="stylesheet" />

or loaded with css

@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Dancing+Script&display=swap');

Specify font name and font family

tailwind.config.js

module.exports = { theme: { extend: { fontFamily: { fancy: ["Dancing Script"], }, }, },
}

font-fancy can be used.

html

<span class="font-fancy">・・・</span>

Instructions for adding google fonts can be found in this article

Putting the class together

Can create a class that summarizes tailwind classes with @apply.

css

.custom-btn { @apply w-20 h-20 m-5 p5 text-center;
}

html

<button class="custom-btn">Button</button>

The @layer determines the priority of the added style. In this case base < components < utilities.

@layer base { h1 { @apply text-2xl; }
} @layer components { h1 { @apply text-4xl; }
} @layer utilities { h1 { @apply text-5xl; }
}

These uses are quoted from document translated by

base

The base layer is for things like reset rules or default styles applied to plain HTML elements.

components

The components layer is for class-based styles that you want to be able to override with utilities.

utilities

The utilities layer is for small, single-purpose classes that should always take precedence over any other styles.

Toggle light/dark mode

dark: specifies class for dark mode

<div class="bg-white dark:bg-black"> ・・・
</div>

How to determine if it is in dark mode

tailwind.config.js

module.exports = { // elements with dark class and below act as dark mode darkMode: "class", // dark mode is determined by os setting (default) // darkMode: "media", }
}

In this case, it operates as a dark mode

<html class="dark"> ・・・ <div class="bg-white dark:bg-black"> ・・・ </div>
</html>

Dynamically add and remove dark classes to switch between dark and light modes

Switch themes

Create the colors primary and primary-text in this example

tailwind.config.js

module.exports = { theme: { extend: { colors: { 　　 "primary": "var(--color-primary)", 　　 "primary-text": "var(--color-primary-text)", } }, },
}

Set color with css

/* default theme */
:root { --color-primary: #ffffff; --color-primary-text: #000000;
} /* alternate-theme */
.custom-theme { --color-primary: #000000; --color-primary-text: #ffffff;
}

Apply by specifying the class name of the theme

<div class="custom-theme"> <button class="bg-primary text-primary-text">Button</button>
</div>

If you make the class name of the theme dynamic, you can switch the theme of the application for each user, for example.

Items that can be specified in tailwind.config.js and their default values

You can check it here.
https://github.com/tailwindlabs/tailwindcss/blob/master/stubs/defaultConfig.stub.js

Items.Title: 在 Next.js 中创建餐厅条形码菜单。 | Create a restaurant barcode menu in Next.js.
Items.Description:
条形码已被证明可以提高产品利用速度，从而提高全球企业的效率。 条码在商业、技术和工业领域使用了近 50 年。

条码由代表二进制数字 0 和 1 的条形组成。二进制数可以表示从 0 到 9 的值，并由数字计算机处理。

本文简要讨论了条码技术在构建餐厅条码菜单中的应用。

完成的项目在CodeSandbox上。 源代码在Github上。

先决条件

本文需要足够的 JavaScript 和 React.js 知识。 项目设置和安装

要创建一个新项目，我们将在终端中运行以下命令：

 npx create - next - app < project - name >

接下来，我们将导航到项目目录并使用以下命令安装react-barcode包：

 cd < project - name > && npm install react - barcode

运行npm run dev将默认在浏览器中的http://localhost:3000启动开发服务器。

什么是反应条形码？

它可以无缝使用，不需要字体。 生成的条码针对打印和屏幕扫描进行了优化。

它可以显示不同的条形码形式，如 svgs、canvas、图像等。它使用一组默认选项值来显示条形码。

构建条形码组件

随着我们的项目完全设置和配置，我们可以开始构建条形码菜单。

首先，我们将在./pages/index.js中创建一个输入元素和一个名为barcode的状态变量。 输入是接受餐厅的 URL，该 URL 将被转换为条形码，然后存储在barcode状态变量中。 随着输入值的变化，输入将触发一个事件来更新barcode值。

我们还将从react-barcode导入条形码组件，以根据我们的输入值创建条形码。

 import React , { useState } from " react " ; import Barcode from " react-barcode " ; export default function Home () { const [ barcode , setBarcode ] = useState ( " CODE128 " ); const handleChange = ( event ) => { setBarcode ( event . target . value ? event . target . value : "" ); }; return ( < div className = " App " > < h1 className = " title " > Restaurant Barcode Menu < /h1 > < input type = " text " placeholder = " type here " onChange = { handleChange } / > < /div > ); }

接下来，我们将添加条形码组件，该组件利用一些关于如何显示条形码的选项。

 < Barcode value = { barcode } format = { " auto " } height = { 90 } width = { 1.5 } fontOptions = " 600 " textMargin = { 4 } margin = { 0 } ref = { barcodeRef } / >

上面代码片段中的选项执行以下操作：

  • 例如CODE128、CODE39、EAN13等。
  • 如果没有设置任何其他内容，所有边都将继承边距属性，但如果我们单独设置它们，则可以替换它们。
  • 我们的条形码菜单在浏览器中应如下所示：

    测试条码菜单

    我们会将随机餐厅的 URL 转换为条形码，以确保条形码菜单功能齐全。

    然后，我们必须借助计算机系统或手机上的任何条码扫描器应用程序来扫描条码。 条形码扫描仪将显示与上面条形码菜单中的 URL 相同的 URL，以表明我们的应用程序功能齐全。

    结论

    本文讨论了如何将 URL 转换为条形码，更重要的是，如何将条形码集成到 Web 应用程序中。

    资源

    • 反应条形码包
    • 反应条形码文档


原文:

Barcodes have a proven to increase the speed to product utilization thereby improving the efficiency of enterprises worldwide. Barcodes are utilized in commerce, technology, and industrial sectors for almost 50 years.

Barcode consists of bars representing the binary digits 0 and 1. The binary numbers can represent values ranging from 0 to 9 and are processed by a digital computer.

This article briefly discusses the application of barcode technology in building a restaurant barcode menu.

Codesandbox and Github

The completed project is on CodeSandbox. Fork it to get started quickly.

<Codesandbox id="friendly-kepler-tb8pcj" title="Create a restaurant barcode menu in Next.js" />

The source code is on Github.

Prerequisite

Adequate knowledge of JavaScript and React.js is required for this article. The knowledge of Next.js is preferred but not required.

Getting Started with Next.js

Next.js is a React-based framework with functionalities like pre-rendering, automatic code-splitting for faster page loads, and API routes to build API endpoints with serverless functions for back-end features.

Created by Vercel, Next.js is open source and based on Node.js and Babel — and also integrates with React to create single-page apps.

Project setup and installation

To create a new project, we’ll run the following command in our terminal:

npx create-next-app <project-name>

Next, we’ll navigate into the project directory and install the react-barcode package with the command below:

cd <project-name> && npm install react-barcode

Running npm run dev will start the development server by default at http://localhost:3000 in our browser.

What is react-barcode?

react-barcode is a lightweight, high-performance component for React applications that display standard 1D and 2D barcodes. It is seamless to use and does not require fonts. The barcodes generated are optimized for printing and on-screen scanning.

react-barcode is easily customizable, as it provides options to customize its color, height, width, and more. It can display different barcodes forms such as svgs, canvas, images, etc. It uses a set of default option values to display a barcode.

Building the barcode component

With our project fully set up and configured, we can start building the barcode menu.

First, we’ll create an input element and a state variable called barcode in ./pages/index.js. The input is to accept the restaurant’s URL, which will be transformed into a barcode and then stored in the barcode state variable. The input will trigger an event to update the barcode value as the input value changes.

We’ll also import the barcode component from react-barcode to create a barcode from our input value.

import React, { useState } from "react";
import Barcode from "react-barcode"; export default function Home() {
const [barcode, setBarcode] = useState("CODE128");
const handleChange = (event) => { setBarcode(event.target.value ? event.target.value : "");
};
return (
<div className="App"> <h1 className="title"> Restaurant Barcode Menu </h1>
 <input type="text" placeholder="type here" onChange={handleChange} />
 </div>
 ); }

Next, we’ll add the barcode component, which utilizes some options on how to display the barcode.

<Barcode value={barcode} format={"auto"} height={90} width={1.5} fontOptions="600" textMargin={4} margin={0} ref={barcodeRef} />

The options in the code snippet above do the following:

  • value - This holds the input value converted to a barcode.
  • format - Select which barcode type to use. For example, CODE128, CODE39, EAN13, etc.
  • height - This is the height of the barcode.
  • width - This is the width of a single bar.
  • fontOptions - This adds a bold or italic text to the barcode.
  • textMargin - This sets the space between the barcode and the text.
  • margin - Sets the space margin around the barcode. All sides will inherit the margins property if nothing else is set but can be replaced if we set them separably.
  • ref: To access the Barcode element after it has been mounted to the DOM.

Our barcode menu should look like this in our browser:

Testing the barcode menu

We’ll transform a random restaurant’s URL into a barcode to ensure the barcode menu is fully functional.

Then, we’ll have to scan the barcode with the help of any barcode scanner application on our computer system or phone. The barcode scanner will show the same URL as the one in the barcode menu above to show our application is fully functional.

Conclusion

This article discussed how to transform URLs into barcodes and, more importantly, integrate barcodes into web applications.

Resources

  • react-barcode package
  • react-barcode Documentation
